content_en: His call spread throughout Syria and Egypt; artisans and craftsmen at the border point of Alexandria took part of their earnings to him, and women sent some of their wealth to him.687 Books and writings were found in his possession that were indicative of blatant disbelief.688 Thus Salah ad-Deen — by the grace of Allah and then by virtue of his patience and strong leadership — was able to put an end to this conspiracy which finally prompted him to make a decisive move with regard to the remnants of the Fatimid state, namely the caliph's family, the senior officials, the courtesans and the Sudanese troops.689 The poet 'Amarah ibn 'Ali al-Yamani Adh-Dhahabi said concerning him that (his name was) Abu Muhammad 'Amarah ibn 'Ali ibn Zaydan al-Hakami al-Madhhaji al-Yamani Al-Shafi'i al-Fardi, the poet and author of the famous Diwdn. He was born in 515 AH and studied Islam in Zubayd for a while. He performed Hajj in 549 AH, and the emir of Makkah, Qasim ibn Faleetah, sent him as a messenger to al-Fa'iz in Egypt, whom he praised in verse. He had a clear and strong belief in Abu Bakr and 'Umar, and 'Amarah narrated that Salih ibn Ruzayk debated with him and said, "What do you believe concerning Abu Bakr and 'Umar?" 'Amarah said, "I believe that were it not for them, there would be no Islam for us and for you, and that loving them is obligatory," and he laughed. He was kind and smart, and learned from the Sunni scholars. Adh-Dhahabi said, "This is a sign of patience on Salih's part, despite the fact that he was a Shiite."690 Ibn Wasil said concerning the reason for 'Amarah al-Yamani's loyalty to the Fatimids: 'Amarah ibn 'Ali al-Yamani was fanatically devoted to them, because he came to them from Yemen and they treated him well and gave him many gifts. So he never forgot that and tried to repay them. As they say, man is beholden to good treatment. He did not follow their madh-hab; rather he was a Shafi'i and Sunni. When their state ended, he eulogized them in the best poetry and defended them verbally, because he was not able to defend them physically. Then when a group took action in an attempt to restore them, he was one of the people who helped that effort, out of gratitude to them for their kind treatment. That led to him being hanged.691 — As we have seen above. 'Amarah mentioned his inclination towards the Fatimids in an ode in which he said: Their generous deeds were like those of the Sunnis, even though they differed from me by holding Shiite beliefs.692 Adh-Dhahabi commented on this poetry, saying: "Would that it had only been Shiism; rather would that it had been only Rafidism. Rather his attitude was one of utter heresy."693 'Amarah said, eulogized the Fatimids and al-'Adid: I grieve for the time of Imam al- 'Adid as the barren woman grieves for the death of her only child. I sat with his viziers and accompanied his emirs, those who deserve eternal praise. I feel sorry for the rooms of your palace which are now devoid, O son of the (Messenger (SAAW), of the crowds of delegates. And for your separation from your troops who were like the waves on the calm sea...694 I am astonished by 'Amarah al-Yamani's eulogy for the Fatimids and his nostalgia for their innovations, festivals and palaces, his challenge to the new Sunni state in Egypt and his defence of the Fatimids and their lies and false claims to be descended from the (Messenger (SAAW) . Does love for the transient conveniences of this world do to sound belief what it did to 'Amarah al-Yamani? Do gifts, status and position make a man give up his sound belief and weep for the demise of the Rafidi, Shiite Fatimid state and get involved with a conspiracy against the Islamic mission to resist the Crusaders in the Muslim lands?
content_id: Seruannya menyebar di seluruh Syam dan Mesir; para pengrajin dan tukang di titik perbatasan Alexandria mengambil sebagian penghasilan mereka untuknya, dan para wanita mengirimkan sebagian kekayaan mereka kepadanya. Buku-buku dan tulisan-tulisan ditemukan dalam kepemilikannya yang mengindikasikan kekufuran yang nyata. Dengan demikian Salahuddin — berkat rahmat Allah kemudian berkat kesabarannya dan kepemimpinannya yang kuat — mampu mengakhiri konspirasi ini yang akhirnya mendorongnya untuk mengambil langkah tegas terkait sisa-sisa negara Fathimiyyin, yaitu keluarga khalifah, pejabat senior, para pelayan istana, dan pasukan Sudan. Penyair 'Amarah ibn 'Ali al-Yamani Adz-Dzahabi mengatakan tentang dia bahwa (namanya) Abu Muhammad 'Amarah ibn 'Ali ibn Zaidan al-Hakami al-Madzhaji al-Yamani asy-Syafi'i al-Fardi, penyair dan pengarang Diwan yang terkenal. Ia lahir pada tahun 515 H dan belajar Islam di Zubayd untuk beberapa waktu. Ia menunaikan haji pada tahun 549 H, dan amir Makkah, Qasim ibn Falaitha, mengirimnya sebagai utusan kepada al-Fa'iz di Mesir, yang ia puji dalam syair. Ia memiliki keyakinan yang jelas dan kuat mengenai Abu Bakr dan Umar, dan 'Amarah meriwayatkan bahwa Shalih ibn Ruzaik berdebat dengannya dan berkata, "Apa keyakinanmu mengenai Abu Bakr dan Umar?" 'Amarah berkata, "Aku berkeyakinan bahwa seandainya bukan karena mereka berdua, tidak ada Islam bagi kami dan bagi kalian, dan bahwa mencintai mereka adalah wajib," dan ia pun tertawa. Ia murah hati dan cerdas, serta belajar dari para ulama Sunni. Adz-Dzahabi berkata, "Ini adalah tanda kesabaran dari pihak Shalih, meskipun ia seorang Syiah." Ibn Wasil berkata mengenai alasan kesetiaan 'Amarah al-Yamani kepada kaum Fathimiyyin: 'Amarah ibn 'Ali al-Yamani sangat fanatik kepada mereka, karena ia datang kepada mereka dari Yaman dan mereka memperlakukannya dengan baik serta memberikan banyak hadiah kepadanya. Sehingga ia tidak pernah melupakan hal itu dan berusaha membalasnya. Seperti kata pepatah, manusia terikat oleh perlakuan baik. Ia tidak mengikuti mazhab mereka; sebaliknya ia bermazhab Syafi'i dan Sunni. Ketika negara mereka berakhir, ia meratapi mereka dalam syair terbaik dan membela mereka secara lisan, karena ia tidak mampu membela mereka secara fisik. Kemudian ketika sekelompok orang mengambil tindakan dalam upaya untuk memulihkan mereka, ia adalah salah satu dari orang-orang yang membantu upaya tersebut, sebagai ungkapan rasa terima kasih kepada mereka atas perlakuan baik mereka. Hal itulah yang menyebabkan ia digantung. 'Amarah menyebutkan kecenderungannya kepada kaum Fathimiyyin dalam sebuah ode di mana ia berkata: Perbuatan murah hati mereka seperti perbuatan kaum Sunni, meskipun mereka berbeda dariku dengan menganut keyakinan Syiah. Adz-Dzahabi mengomentari syair ini, mengatakan: "Seandainya hanya Syiah saja; bahkan seandainya hanya Rafidhah saja. Bahkan sikapnya adalah kemurtadan yang mutlak." 'Amarah berkata, meratapi kaum Fathimiyyin dan al-'Adid: Aku berduka atas masa Imam al-'Adid seperti perempuan mandul berduka atas kematian anak tunggalnya. Aku duduk bersama para wazirnya dan menemani para amirnya, mereka yang layak mendapatkan pujian abadi. Aku merasa sedih atas kamar-kamar istanamu yang kini kosong, wahai putra Rasulullah, dari kerumunan para delegasi. Dan atas perpisahanmu dari pasukan-pasukanmu yang bagaikan ombak di lautan yang tenang... Aku heran dengan ratapan 'Amarah al-Yamani untuk kaum Fathimiyyin dan kerinduannya akan bid'ah, perayaan, dan istana-istana mereka, tantangannya terhadap negara Sunni baru di Mesir, serta pembelaannya terhadap kaum Fathimiyyin dan kebohongan serta klaim palsu mereka sebagai keturunan Rasulullah. Apakah kecintaan pada kemudahan dunia yang fana melakukan pada keyakinan yang benar apa yang dilakukannya pada 'Amarah al-Yamani? Apakah hadiah, status, dan jabatan dapat membuat seseorang melepaskan keyakinannya yang benar dan menangisi lenyapnya negara Fathimiyyin Rafidhah Syiah serta terlibat dalam konspirasi melawan misi Islam untuk melawan kaum Salib di tanah-tanah Muslim?
